description: Defines how KeyMemory is used for project handoff, subproject continuity, and cross-agent relay without becoming the authoritative source of project facts.

judgment.reason: Large projects need continuity across sessions, agents, and subprojects. KeyMemory is good for compact state and retrieval pointers, while file-backed project knowledge is better for auditable facts.

relay_memory_schema.title: required
relay_memory_schema.source_refs: list of project knowledge entry ids, filenames, reports, or user confirmations

write_rules: Write relay memory when an Agent hands off to another Agent.
Write relay memory when a subproject finishes or blocks.
Write relay memory when the user confirms an important project decision that is not yet in a formal document.
Do not write raw document text, credentials, or unapproved methodology drafts to KeyMemory.
Every relay memory must include source_refs when it mentions facts.
read_rules: Use relay memories to reconstruct project state quickly.
Validate fact-critical relay memories against project source documents before final answers.
If relay memory conflicts with project documents, report the conflict and prefer the project documents.
Treat relay memories without source_refs as orientation, not evidence.

gui_contract.backend_action: The GUI backend should sync outbox entries to KeyMemory using MCP or REST, then mark them synced.
